summary:
• Strait of Hormuz volatility—including Iranian clashes, ADNOC's "dark mode" LNG operations, and accelerating alternative shipping routes through Eurasia—is creating structural supply-route risk for Western Hemisphere exporters competing for Asian markets.

• Geopolitical realignment away from traditional suppliers is intensifying, with India, China, and Asia pivoting toward Russia, Brazil, and Venezuela while U.S. sanctions on Venezuelan oil paradoxically strengthen non-Western supply chains and reduce Americas market leverage.

• Arctic and unconventional production face competing headwinds from rising climate constraints in Europe and production challenges in Russia, while U.S. onshore growth (Alaska, Duvernay) and offshore expansion (Ivoire) are critical offsets to maintain Western Hemisphere supply competitiveness against demand shifts toward EVs and alternative energy.
